g.text: ## Unterminated Construct Error Position: Opening Delimiter vs Current Position
Error token position = snapshot at `_begin_token()`, NOT position at detection

text.tspan: ## Unterminated Construct Error Position: Opening Delimiter vs Current Position
Error token position = snapshot at `_begin_token()`, NOT position at detection

g.text: **before** any character of the token is consumed.
They remain **frozen** for the entire duration of scanning that token.
and describe "where the cursor is NOW" — not "where the token started."
For unterminated constructs the detection point is always **after** the
opening delimiter — sometimes many lines later. The error MUST reference
the opening delimiter position. Only `token_start_*` preserves that.

text.tspan: **before** any character of the token is consumed.
They remain **frozen** for the entire duration of scanning that token.
and describe "where the cursor is NOW" — not "where the token started."
For unterminated constructs the detection point is always **after** the
opening delimiter — sometimes many lines later. The error MUST reference
the opening delimiter position. Only `token_start_*` preserves that.

That is WHERE THE PROBLEM IS

g.text: `x` on line 1 | `/*` opens on line 2 | content on line 3

text.tspan: `x` on line 1 | `/*` opens on line 2 | content on line 3

g.text: If we used current position instead
**Error points to line 2, col 1** — the opening `/`
That is WHERE THE PROBLEM IS
